Short Title of 2006 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 109–460, § 1(a)120 Stat. 3401Dec. 22, 2006“This section [amending sections 467d, 467f, and 467j of this title] may be cited as the ‘Dam Safety Act of 2006’.”

Short Title of 2002 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 107–310, § 1(a)116 Stat. 2450Dec. 2, 2002“This Act [enacting and amending this section and sections 467e to 467g and 467h to 467j of this title] may be cited as the ‘Dam Safety and Security Act of 2002’.”section 467g–1 of this title

Short Title of 1986 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 99–662, title XII, § 1206100 Stat. 4264Nov. 17, 1986“This title [enacting sections 467f to 467n and 2311 of this title and amending this section and sections 467a and 467b of this title] may be cited as the ‘Dam Safety Act of 1986’.”

Short Title, as added by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 92–367, § 1Pub. L. 104–303, title II, § 215(c)(1)110 Stat. 3685Oct. 12, 1996“This Act [enacting this subchapter] may be cited as the ‘National Dam Safety Program Act’.”

Transfer of FunctionsFor transfer of all functions, personnel, assets, components, authorities, grant programs, and liabilities of the Federal Emergency Management Agency, including the functions of the Under Secretary for Federal Emergency Management relating thereto, to the Federal Emergency Management Agency, see , Domestic Security.section 315(a)(1) of Title 6For transfer of functions, personnel, assets, and liabilities of the Federal Emergency Management Agency, including the functions of the Administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency relating thereto, to the Secretary of Homeland Security, and for treatment of related references, see former section 313(1) and sections 551(d), 552(d), and 557 of Title 6, Domestic Security, and the Department of Homeland Security Reorganization Plan of , as modified, set out as a note under .November 25, 2002section 542 of Title 6

Congressional Statement of Purpose; National Dam Safety Program, , , provided that:Pub. L. 104–303, title II, § 215(a)110 Stat. 3685Oct. 12, 1996“The purpose of this section [enacting this section and sections 467d to 467j of this title, amending sections 467a to 467c of this title and , Indians, repealing former sections 467 and 467d to 467m of this title, and enacting provisions set out as notes under this section] is to reduce the risks to life and property from dam failure in the United States through the establishment and maintenance of an effective national dam safety program to bring together the expertise and resources of the Federal and non-Federal communities in achieving national dam safety hazard reduction. It is not the intent of this section to preempt any other Federal or State authorities nor is it the intent of this section to mandate State participation in the grant assistance program to be established under this section.”section 3802 of Title 25

Effect on Other Dam Safety Programs, , , provided that:Pub. L. 104–303, title II, § 215(b)110 Stat. 3685Oct. 12, 1996“Nothing in this section (including the amendments made by this section) [enacting this section and sections 467d to 467j of this title, amending sections 467a to 467c of this title and , Indians, repealing former sections 467 and 467d to 467m of this title, and enacting provisions set out as notes under this section] shall preempt or otherwise affect any dam safety program of a Federal agency other than the Federal Emergency Management Agency, including any program that regulates, permits, or licenses any activity affecting a dam.”section 3802 of Title 25

children.heading: Administrator
children.label: 1 Administrator
children.text: The term “Administrator” means the Administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency.

children.heading: Board
children.label: 2 Board
children.text: The term “Board” means a National Dam Safety Review Board established under .section 467f(f) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: the natural bed of the stream channel or watercourse measured at the downstream toe of the barrier; or

children.label: II
children.text: if the barrier is not across a stream channel or watercourse, from the lowest elevation of the outside limit of the barrier;

children.label: ii
children.text: has an impounding capacity for maximum storage elevation of 50 acre-feet or more; but

children.label: i
children.text: a levee; or

children.label: I
children.text: is 6 feet or less in height regardless of storage capacity; or

children.label: II
children.text: has a storage capacity at the maximum water storage elevation that is 15 acre-feet or less regardless of height;

children.label: i
children.text: is located in a State with a State dam safety program;

children.label: ii
children.text: is classified as “high hazard potential” by the State dam safety agency in the State in which the dam is located;

children.label: I
children.text: is approved by the relevant State dam safety agency; or

children.label: II
children.text: is in conformance with State law and pending approval by the relevant State dam safety agency;

children.label: iv
children.text: fails to meet minimum dam safety standards of the State in which the dam is located, as determined by the State; and

children.label: v
children.text: poses an unacceptable risk to the public, as determined by the Administrator, in consultation with the Board.

children.label: i
children.text: a licensed hydroelectric dam under a hydropower project with an authorized installed capacity of greater than 1.5 megawatts; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a dam built under the authority of the Secretary of Agriculture.

children.heading: Federal agency
children.label: 5 Federal agency
children.text: The term “Federal agency” means a Federal agency that designs, finances, constructs, owns, operates, maintains, or regulates the construction, operation, or maintenance of a dam.

children.heading: Federal Guidelines for Dam Safety
children.label: 6 Federal Guidelines for Dam Safety
children.text: The term “Federal Guidelines for Dam Safety” means the FEMA publication, numbered 93 and dated June 1979, that defines management practices for dam safety at all Federal agencies.

children.heading: FEMA
children.label: 7 FEMA
children.text: The term “FEMA” means the Federal Emergency Management Agency.

children.heading: Hazard reduction
children.label: 8 Hazard reduction
children.text: The term “hazard reduction” means the reduction in the potential consequences to life and property of dam failure.

children.heading: ICODS
children.label: 9 ICODS
children.text: The term “ICODS” means the Interagency Committee on Dam Safety established by .section 467e of this title

children.label: A
children.text: a governmental organization; and

children.label: B
children.text: a nonprofit organization.

children.heading: Program
children.label: 11 Program
children.text: The term “Program” means the national dam safety program established under .section 467f of this title

children.heading: Rehabilitation
children.label: 12 Rehabilitation
children.text: The term “rehabilitation” means the repair, replacement, reconstruction, or removal of a dam that is carried out to meet applicable State dam safety and security standards.

children.heading: State
children.label: 13 State
children.text: The term “State” means each of the several States of the United States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands, Guam, American Samoa, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, and any other territory or possession of the United States.

children.heading: State dam safety agency
children.label: 14 State dam safety agency
children.text: The term “State dam safety agency” means a State agency that has regulatory authority over the safety of non-Federal dams.

children.heading: State dam safety program
children.label: 15 State dam safety program
children.text: The term “State dam safety program” means a State dam safety program approved and assisted under .section 467f(e) of this title

children.heading: Underserved community
children.label: 16 Underserved community
children.text: The term “underserved community” means a community with a population of less than 50,000 that has a median household income of less than 80 percent of the statewide median household income.

children.heading: United States
children.label: 17 United States
children.text: The term “United States”, when used in a geographical sense, means all of the States.
